mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings growth and supports S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month. This relationship is persistent across the business cycle.
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns show negative correlation with RSAFS over any rolling 3-year window, or the signal fails to produce positive average returns in top RSAFS quintile periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.5%, it signals labor market weakness that historically precedes economic contractions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In episodes where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, forward 20-day SPX returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously low interest rates (FEDFUNDS below 2.0) and strong retail sales (RSAFS above 490,000) create a powerful tailwind for equities by combining cheap financing with robust consumer demand, amplifying positive forward returns.
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive (one-sample t-test vs zero, p<0.05) or the interaction term in a linear regression is not significant (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a low nominal interest rate environment (FEDFUNDS below 2.0), rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals strong real economic activity that translates directly into higher equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS is below 2.0, the correlation between INDPRO changes and forward 60-day SPX returns is negative or insignificant (|rank IC| < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production (INDPRO) reflect expanding output and capacity utilization, which historically drives corporate profits and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day forward horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year rank IC of INDPRO changes vs forward 60-day SPX returns is below 0.05, or the long-short portfolio based on INDPRO deciles produces negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,200 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and consumer confidence, a leading indicator of economic slowdown that produces negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods with HOUST below 1200, forward 20-day SPX returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not statistically negative at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and rising retail sales (RSAFS) confirms a self-reinforcing cycle of job growth and consumer spending, which boosts corporate earnings and lifts stock prices over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The product of changes in PAYEMS and RSAFS (interaction) has no significant positive relationship with forward 20-day SPX returns in a regression controlling for individual effects (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary easing cycle (defined by FEDFUNDS declining over the past three months), further declines in FEDFUNDS compress discount rates and boost equity valuations over a short horizon of one week.
failure_condition: During easing cycles, the correlation between week-over-week FEDFUNDS changes and forward 5-day SPX returns is not negative (i.e., not consistent with the expected positive direction), or the average return is not positive.
